matchQuestion: If \[\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \frac{{\cos \left( {2x} \right) + a\cos \left( {4x} \right) - b}}{{{x^4}}}\]is finite, then (a+b) is equal to :
matchSolution:
\[\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \frac{{\cos 2x + a\cos 4x - b}}{{{x^4}}} = finite\]
\[L = \frac{{\left\{ {1 - \frac{{{{\left( {2x} \right)}^2}}}{{2!}} + \frac{{{{\left( {2x} \right)}^4}}}{{4!}}} \right\} + a\left\{ {1 - \frac{{{{\left( {4x} \right)}^2}}}{{2!}} + \frac{{{{\left( {4x} \right)}^4}}}{{4!}}} \right\} - b}}{{{x^4}}}\]
\[L = \frac{{\left( {1 + a - b} \right) - {x^2}\left( {2 + 8a} \right) + {x^4}\left( {\frac{2}{3} + \frac{{32}}{3}a} \right) + {x^6}()......}}{{{x^4}}}\]
\[\therefore 1 + a - b = 0\]and \[2 + 8a = 0 \Rightarrow a = - \frac{1}{4}\]
\[b = a + 1\]
\[ = - \frac{1}{4} + 1 = \frac{3}{4}\]
\[\therefore a + b = - \frac{1}{4} + \frac{3}{4} = \frac{1}{2}\]

Now the acid solution is diluted with excess of water so that the pH of the solution changes to 6. The new concentration of the diluted weak acid is given as x × 10<sup>–4</sup> M. The value of x is ________(nearest integer)

matchQuestion: Let m and n be the number of points at which the function ƒ(x) = max{x, x<sup>3</sup>, x<sup>5</sup>,….., x<sup>21</sup>}, x ∈  ℝ, is not differentiable and not continuous , respectively. Then m + n is equal to ________.
matchSolution:
\[f(x) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {x,}&{x < - 1} \\
  {{x^{21}},}&{ - 1 \leqslant x < 0} \\
  {x,}&{0 \leqslant x < 1} \\
  {{x^{21}},}&{x \geqslant 1}
\end{array}} \right.\]
f(x) is continuous everywhere.
∴ n = 0
\[f(x) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {1,}&{x < - 1} \\
  {21{x^{20}},}&{ - 1 \leqslant x < 0} \\
  {1,}&{0 \leqslant x < 1} \\
  {21{x^{20}},}&{x \geqslant 1}
\end{array}} \right.\]
∴ f(x) is non – differentiable at x = − 1, 0, 1
∴ m = 3
m + n = 3

matchQuestion: If \[\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \frac{{\cos \left( {2x} \right) + a\cos \left( {4x} \right) - b}}{{{x^4}}}\]is finite, then (a+b) is equal to :
matchSolution:
\[\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \frac{{\cos 2x + a\cos 4x - b}}{{{x^4}}} = finite\]
\[L = \frac{{\left\{ {1 - \frac{{{{\left( {2x} \right)}^2}}}{{2!}} + \frac{{{{\left( {2x} \right)}^4}}}{{4!}}} \right\} + a\left\{ {1 - \frac{{{{\left( {4x} \right)}^2}}}{{2!}} + \frac{{{{\left( {4x} \right)}^4}}}{{4!}}} \right\} - b}}{{{x^4}}}\]
\[L = \frac{{\left( {1 + a - b} \right) - {x^2}\left( {2 + 8a} \right) + {x^4}\left( {\frac{2}{3} + \frac{{32}}{3}a} \right) + {x^6}()......}}{{{x^4}}}\]
\[\therefore 1 + a - b = 0\]and \[2 + 8a = 0 \Rightarrow a = - \frac{1}{4}\]
\[b = a + 1\]
\[ = - \frac{1}{4} + 1 = \frac{3}{4}\]
\[\therefore a + b = - \frac{1}{4} + \frac{3}{4} = \frac{1}{2}\]

matchQuestion:
Let the range of the function
\[f(x) = 6 + 16\,\,\,\,\cos x.\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} - x} \right).\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + x} \right).\]
sin3x . cos6x, x ∈ R be [α, β]. Then the distance of the point (α, β) from the line 3x + 4y + 12 = 0 is :
matchSolution:
\[f(x) = 6 + 16\left( {\frac{1}{4}\cos 3x} \right)\sin 3x.\cos 6x\]
= 6 + 4 cos3x sin3x cos6x
= 6 + sin12x
Range of f(x) is [5, 7]
(α , β ) ≡ (5, 7)
distance = \[\left| {\frac{{15 + 28 + 12}}{5}} \right| = 11\]

Phenol gives violet colour with neutral $\mathrm{FeCl}_{3}$ not ethanol.
- $1^{\circ}$ amine gives positive test with $\mathrm{CHCl}_{3} / \mathrm{KOH}$ not $2^{\circ}$ amine.
- Aldehyde gives positive test with ammonical silver nitrate not ketone.
- Cinnamic acid gives bromine water test.
